Configuration options for enabling and setting parameters of the Jerryscript ECMAScript 5.1 interpreter.

# Purpose
The configuration file defines settings for integrating the Jerryscript ECMAScript 5.1 interpreter into a software system. The `JERRYSCRIPT` option is a boolean that enables or disables the Jerryscript interpreter. When enabled, additional configurations become available, such as `JERRYSCRIPT_PROGNAME`, which specifies the program name used when the NSH ELF program is installed, and it depends on the `BUILD_KERNEL` option. The file also sets the task priority, stack size, and heap size for Jerryscript through `JERRYSCRIPT_PRIORITY`, `JERRYSCRIPT_STACKSIZE`, and `JERRYSCRIPT_HEAPSIZE`, respectively, with default values of 100, 16384, and 107520. These configurations allow users to customize the behavior and resource allocation of the Jerryscript interpreter within the system.
